name: Bear's Path Greenway
name_original: Proaza, Desdiladero de Peñas Juntas en la Senda del Oso, Embalse de Valdemurrio, Bárzana – ida y vuelta al lado del río Trubia
description: This 19.1-mile mountain bike route on the Senda del Oso takes you through the Peñas Juntas Gorge and past Valdemurrio Reservoir.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Proaza are set within Asturias, Spain, a region characterized by rugged peaks, deep river valleys, and dramatic gorges. The landscape features the Trubia River, which carves through verdant terrain, and significant mountain formations like Peña Sobia and the Sierra de Tameza. Riparian forests of oaks and alders line the riverbanks, providing a diverse environment for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Proaza The most popular mountain bike trail is Bear's Path Greenway, a 19.1 miles (30.8 km) difficult route that takes about 2 hours 39 minutes to complete. This trail follows a former mining railway, passing through tunnels and spectacular gorges. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Peñas Juntas Gorge – Stone Bridge of Proaza loop from Villanueva, a difficult 22.1 miles (35.5 km) path. This route offers views of the impressive Peñas Juntas Gorge and the surrounding mountainous terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Mine Railway Tunnel – Senda del Oso loop from Proaza, a 13.3 miles (21.3 km) trail leading through a former railway tunnel and along the Senda del Oso, often completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. Mountain biking around Proaza is defined by rugged peaks, river valleys, and greenways built on former railway lines. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family-friendly sections to more challenging routes. The routes in Proaza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 1,200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Proaza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Proaza?
A: Proaza offers a diverse network of over 40 mountain bike trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy greenways to more challenging mountain paths, all set within the stunning Asturian landscape.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for families or beginners in Proaza?
A: Yes, Proaza is well-known for its family-friendly options. The &lt;strong&gt;Senda del Oso&lt;/strong&gt; (Bear's Path) is an excellent choice, built on a former mining railway, offering an accessible and scenic ride. A particularly easy section is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31434851" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mine Railway Tunnel – Senda del Oso loop from Proaza&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.3 miles (21.3 km) long and takes about 1 hour 40 minutes.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes in Proaza?
A: For those seeking a challenge, Proaza offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027486462/bear-s-path-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bear's Path Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, while having easier sections, is rated difficult overall at 19.1 miles (30.8 km) due to its length and elevation changes. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31432606" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas Juntas Gorge – Stone Bridge of Proaza loop from Villanueva&lt;/a&gt;, a 22.1 miles (35.5 km) path with significant climbs and descents.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on mountain bike trails in Proaza?
A: Proaza's mountain bike trails offer breathtaking scenery, characterized by rugged peaks, deep river valleys, and dramatic gorges. You'll ride alongside the Trubia River through lush riparian forests of oaks and alders, pass through old railway tunnels, and witness impressive rock formations like those in the Peñas Juntas Gorge. The region is also home to diverse wildlife, including Cantabrian brown bears.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Proaza?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31434851" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mine Railway Tunnel – Senda del Oso loop from Proaza&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31432653" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas Juntas Gorge – Spectacular View loop from La Plaza&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 22.2 km and offers stunning vistas.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions to see while mountain biking in Proaza?
A: Beyond the trails themselves, Proaza boasts several points of interest. You can explore the medieval Tower of Proaza or the historic village of Banduxu. Natural attractions include the impressive Peñas Juntas Gorge and the Route of Las Xanas Gorge. For wildlife enthusiasts, the 'Valley of the Bear' is a refuge for Cantabrian brown bears, and you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/347031" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Alba&lt;/a&gt; for a cultural stop.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Proaza?
A: The mountain bike trails in Proaza are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 1,200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the scenic beauty, the well-maintained Senda del Oso, and the diverse options for all skill levels.

Q: Is mountain bike rental available in Proaza?
A: Yes, bicycle and Segway rentals are available in the Proaza area, particularly along the Senda del Oso, making it convenient for visitors who don't bring their own equipment.

Q: Are there parking facilities at the trailheads in Proaza?
A: While specific parking locations are not detailed for every trailhead, Proaza is a popular outdoor destination, and parking is generally available near key access points for the Senda del Oso and other popular routes. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Proaza?
A: Proaza, located in Asturias, experiences a mild climate. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking, with comfortable temperatures and lush landscapes. Summer can also be good, though it might be warmer, while winter can bring colder temperatures and potentially snow at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any routes that explore former mining areas?
A: Yes, the region has a rich mining history. The famous Senda del Oso (Bear's Path) is built upon a former mining railway, and routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20496857" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pista de Ricabo – Santa Marina Mines loop from Las Agüeras&lt;/a&gt; specifically take you through areas with historical mining significance, offering a unique blend of natural beauty and industrial heritage.
tips: Its strategic location made it a key point in the communication between Asturias and the Meseta in the Middle Ages. Proaza preserves abundant and striking vestiges from that period, such as the medieval Tower of Proaza. Proaza is a very popular place with hikers and cyclists who follow the Senda del Oso, one of the most outstanding green and ecological routes in the area.
Mandatory stop. It has a walkway that crosses the Senda del Oso. It is a very beautiful place with wonderful views. There is a bar if you fancy something (closes at 7pm).
A gorge so narrow that there are walls almost stuck to each other. It is a wonderful place and there are hazelnut, beech and riverside forests in the area.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of mountain biking this route?
faqs.answer: While komoot rates this route as 'difficult' due to its length and elevation gain, the trail itself, being part of the Senda del Oso, is largely considered easy with gentle gradients, typically not exceeding 4%. It's built on a former railway line, making it suitable for cyclists of varying skill levels, including families. However, some sections might be uneven or narrow, so good cycling skills are beneficial.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The route generally features a well-conditioned and mostly paved surface. You'll traverse numerous tunnels, some illuminated, and old iron bridges crossing rivers like the Trubia. The path runs along the valley floor, flanked by limestone and quartzite rock slopes, and meanders through areas of riverside forests, oak groves, and chestnut trees. Some variations or offshoots might involve unpaved or rocky sections.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter stunning natural beauty and historical elements. Key highlights include the spectacular Peñas Juntas Gorge, the picturesque Valdemurio Reservoir, and the Medieval Tower of Proaza. The Trubia River accompanies the path for much of the way, offering beautiful riverside views.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Senda del Oso is generally dog-friendly. However, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times, especially since the trail passes through the habitat of the endangered Cantabrian brown bear. Always be respectful of wildlife and other trail users.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is renowned for its lush vegetation and dense forests, making spring and autumn particularly beautiful with vibrant colors. Summer offers warmer weather, but can be busy. Winter conditions might include colder temperatures and potentially wet surfaces, but the trail remains accessible. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the natural park?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to access the Senda del Oso trail itself. The route is located within the Las Ubiñas-La Mesa Natural Park, but general access to the park for activities like mountain biking does not require a permit. Always follow Leave No Trace principles and park regulations.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: You can find parking options in towns along the Senda del Oso. For this specific route, starting from Proaza is a common choice, where there are designated parking areas. Other access points like Bárzana also offer parking. Bike rental services and transport options are readily available in Proaza.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route largely follows the Vía Verde Senda del Oso. It also intersects with sections of the Asturias Interior (Etapa 15), Sendero Histórico: Asturias, and Asturias Interior (Etapa 14) trails, offering opportunities to explore further if you wish.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through the habitat of the endangered Cantabrian brown bear. A significant attraction is the bear enclosure in Proaza, where these animals can often be observed. You may also spot various bird species and other local fauna in the lush riverside forests.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for mountain biking this 30.8 km out-and-back route is approximately 2 hours and 40 minutes (9578 seconds) of moving time. This can vary depending on your pace, stops for photos, and exploration of points of interest.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like cafes or water stops along the trail?
faqs.answer: Being a popular greenway, you'll find services in the towns along the route, such as Proaza and Bárzana. These towns offer cafes, restaurants, and opportunities to refill water. It's always advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for the sections between towns.
